title: Transfection of Cas9 RNP (ribonucleoprotein) into adherent cells using the Lipofectamine® RNAiMAX
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"a4o2c","text":"Cas9 nuclease may be used in vivo to create targeted genome modifications.  There are several ways in which to introduce Cas9-guide RNA complexes into cells.  Here we present a method for the transfection of Cas9 RNP’s into HEK293 FT cells using Thermo Fisher Lipofectamine® RNAiMAX.  This is a ‘reverse transfection’ method that uses a final concentration of 10 nM RNP per transfection in a 96-well culture plate.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":26,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"972bt","text":"We strongly recommend wearing gloves and using nuclease-free tubes and reagents to avoid RNase contamination. Further recommendations for avoiding ribonuclease contamination can be found here: https://www.neb.com/tools-and-resources/usage-guidelines/avoiding-ribonuclease-contamination","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":193,"length":92}],"data":{}},{"key":"bv09t","text":"Transfection conditions may be highly variable.  It is recommended to optimize your conditions for each cell type and Cas9 target you may have.  This protocol follows conditions that have been optimized for a particular target and use of HEK293 cells.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fu6no","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"57d8t","text":"Seed the cells so that they will be around 70-90% confluent on the day of transfection.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":87},{"style":"italic","offset":43,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"F3253BE739C1410A9C83F4292D50F0AA","url":"https://www.neb.com/tools-and-resources/usage-guidelines/avoiding-ribonuclease-contamination"}}}}

steps: Make a 3 micromolar (µM), working solution of sgRNA by diluting the stock with nuclease-free water.
Make a 3 micromolar (µM), working solution of Cas9-NLS by diluting with 1 X, Cas9 Reaction Buffer or Optimem.

Form the RNP complexes as follows below: A B C 1 Component Single Reaction x3.3 (triplicates) 2 sgRNA (3 μM) 0.5 μl 1.65 μl 3 EnGen Cas9 NLS (3 μM) 0.5 μl 1.65 μl 4 Optimem 11.5 μl 37.95 μl 5 Total 12.5 μl 41.25 μl Component Single Reaction x3.3 (triplicates) sgRNA (3 uM) 0.5 ul 1.65 ul EnGen Cas9 NLS (3 uM) 0.5 ul 1.65 ul Optimem 11.5 37.95 ul Total 12.5 ul 41.25 ul
Gently mix the reaction and incubate at Room temperature for 00:10:00 .

Form the liposome complexes as follows below. A B C 1 Component Single Reaction x3.3 (triplicates) 2 RNP (120 nM) 12.5 µl 41.25 µl 3 RNAiMAX 1.2 µl 3.96 µl 4 Optimem 11.3 µl 37.29 µl 5 Total 12.5 µl 82.5 µl Component Single Reaction x3.3 (triplicates) RNP (120 nM) 12.5 µl 41.25 µl RNAiMAX 1.2 µl 3.96 µl 11.3 µl 37.29 µl 12.5 µl 82.5 µl You can make a master mix of the RNAiMAX and Optimem and add this directly to the RNP tube from above.
Gently mix the reaction and incubate at Room temperature for 00:20:00 .
Seed the cells so that they will be around 70-90% confluent on the day of transfection.
During the RNP/liposome incubation, trypsinize the cells, washing once to remove any traces of trypsin.
Resuspend the cells in 10 mL of media and count.
Calculate the dilution and volume needed to get the cells to 3.2 x 10 5 cells per ml. You will need 125 µL of cells per well.
From each tube of RNP/liposome complex, aliquot 25 µL into 3 wells of a 96-well plate.
Add 125 µL, cells (3.2 x 10 5 cells/ml) to each well containing RNP/liposome complex and pipette up and down gently a few times.
Incubate the cells in a humidified 37 °C , 5% CO 2 incubator for 48-72 hours.
Gently aspirate the media from the cells and wash twice with 100 µL, 1X PBS .
Add 75 µL, Epicentre QuickExtract™ DNA Extraction Solution and shake/vortex for 00:05:00 .
Transfer the solution to a PCR plate or tubes and place in a thermocycler, running the following program: 65 °C for 00:15:00 95 °C for 00:15:00 Hold at 4 °C
Dilute the DNA 1:10 in nuclease-free water.
Follow the protocol detailed in the EnGen Mutation Detection Kit (E3321S) manual .
creator: Breton Hornblower
